description:
# Why

bl-ffb4 introduced a second valid state-checkout layout: .balls/state-repo/ when master_url is set in committed config. bl doctor's drift checks all predate this and assume .balls/worktree/ — so on a master_url-configured repo with a missing/corrupt state-repo, doctor produces diagnostics that point at the wrong path (or report the absence of .balls/worktree/ as a problem when it's correctly absent).

Doctor is the "named reason this repo can't run bl" tool. Wrong diagnostics here defeat its only job.

# What needs to change

- doctor.rs reads config first, branches on master_url to decide which checkout to inspect
- the "state worktree missing" diagnostic names the right path (.balls/state-repo when applicable) and the right fixing command (bl prime auto-provisions in master_url mode; setup_state_branch is the legacy path)
- a doctor check that's specific to master_url mode: does the state-repo's `origin` URL match committed master_url? A user-edited config field that diverges from the materialized clone is exactly the kind of drift doctor exists to surface

# Scope guardrail

Read-only, as doctor always is. Repair stays the only action verb (bl-ffb4's auto_provision_master handles the materialize-on-discover path; doctor just reports). Per the user's standing memory: `repair` is the only action verb in this surface.
title: bl doctor: teach it about .balls/state-repo (master_url mode)
